source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Frank and Frances Carpenter Collection
artist: Unknown photographer (Frank and Frances Carpenter Collection, LoC)
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Totem pole on grave of Tongass George, Ketchikan, Alaska
description: Circa 1900 photograph of a white-painted memorial totem pole marking the grave of Tongass George near Ketchikan, Alaska. The pole is carved with a stylized bear or wolf clan figure and was inscribed in the lower corner 'Totem on Grave of Tongass George, Ketchikan, Alaska'. Grave totems of this kind were raised across Southeast Alaska by Tlingit, Haida, and Tsimshian peoples to honor prominent clan members.
alt: 1900 sepia photograph of a white-painted memorial totem pole with a bear or wolf clan figure carved at the top, marking the grave of Tongass George near Ketchikan
